commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No narrow automated repair remains; maintainer review should decide whether the authenticated ChatGPT file-download boundary and current proof are enough to merge.; Cleared: No concrete security defect remains after the allowlist and scoped fallback updates; the authenticated ChatGPT download boundary is still merge-risk material. (sha=5f06db907f81788ae5299a8f61173afdf3b265aa)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds ChatGPT browser generic file artifact discovery and saving, file artifact metadata, archive gating, docs, and regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the underlying user problem: current main has no generic file artifact kind or ChatGPT file downloader. I did not run a live ChatGPT browser session during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair remains; maintainer review should decide whether the authenticated ChatGPT file-download boundary and current proof are enough to merge.

target.title: [codex] save ChatGPT browser file artifacts
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the narrow file-artifact boundary proposed in steipete/oracle#244:

- add `kind: "file"` session artifacts for ChatGPT browser runs
- discover downloadable file anchors from the assistant response and captured answer text/transcript
- restrict authenticated ChatGPT downloads to known file artifact endpoints only:
  - `/backend-api/files/<id>/download|content`
  - `/backend-api/estuary/content?id=file_*`
  - `/backend-api/sandbox/download?path=/mnt/data/...`
- reject non-file ChatGPT backend APIs such as `/backend-api/me` and conversation endpoints before sending cookies
- recover scoped ChatGPT behavior buttons such as `Download the CSV` when a detected current-turn file candidate cannot be fetched directly
- avoid download-button polling when no file candidate exists, and do not click global/stale page-level download buttons
- include saved files in session artifact metadata and keep the archive guard from archiving before required artifacts are saved

This intentionally does not fetch arbitrary external links or arbitrary ChatGPT `/backend-api/*` URLs from the answer.

## Why

After #169, browser sessions persist transcripts, Deep Research reports, and generated images, but generic ChatGPT-generated files such as ZIPs, wheels, source distributions, CSVs, or PDFs were still stranded in the live ChatGPT conversation. This extends the same artifact layer without adding a new public API.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run tests/browser/chatgptFiles.test.ts`
  - 10 tests passed
- `pnpm exec vitest run tests/browser/artifacts.test.ts tests/browser/chatgptFiles.test.ts tests/browser/chatgptImages.test.ts tests/browser/sessionRunner.test.ts tests/cli/sessionRunner.test.ts`
  - 69 tests passed
- `pnpm run check`
- `pnpm run build`
- `git diff --check`

## Live proof status

Component-level real browser smoke passed against an existing ChatGPT conversation that had a real generated CSV download button. The sandbox URL intentionally returned 404 first, then the scoped button fallback clicked the live ChatGPT `Download the CSV` control and saved the file as an Oracle session artifact:

```text
[browser] Found 1 downloadable file link(s) in captured answer text.
[browser] Failed to save downloadable file 1/1: download failed: 404
[browser] Found 1 downloadable file candidate(s).
[browser] Clicked 1 assistant download button(s).
[browser] Saved 1 downloadable file artifact(s).
```

Redacted saved artifact:

```text
~/.oracle/sessions/pr245-component-live-smoke-1780731237320/artifacts/oracle_pr245_file_artifact_smoke_out(7).csv
mimeType: text/csv
sizeBytes: 26
sourceUrl: browser-download
```

No-file browser proof after the fallback gating fix completed in 20.9s and did not run the download-button fallback:

```text
Session: pr245-no-file-fallback-proof-2
[browser] Saved transcript artifact to ~/.oracle/sessions/pr245-no-file-fallback-proof-2/artifacts/transcript.md
Answer:
OK_NO_FILE_ARTIFACT_PROOF

20.9s · gpt-5.5-pro[browser]
```

The no-file proof log contains no `No assistant download buttons found for button fallback` line and no 60s fallback delay.

Full fresh Oracle E2E generated-file proof was attempted with session `pr245-file-artifact-live-proof-5`. I am **not** counting it as a successful E2E file-artifact proof: ChatGPT returned a text-only `sandbox:/mnt/data/...` link and no real downloadable file/button for that prompt, so the session correctly completed with transcript only. The log shows the late transcript recovery pass running, but there was still no actual file to save:

```text
[browser] No assistant download buttons found for button fallback.
[browser] Retrying downloadable file collection from saved transcript.
[browser] No assistant download buttons found for button fallback.
```

This means the authenticated URL boundary, scoped button-download collector, and no-file fast path are now covered. A maintainer/Mantis proof with a ChatGPT run that actually creates a fresh file artifact would still be useful final merge evidence.

## Notes

- The archive guard now requires detected browser file artifacts to be saved before auto-archiving, matching the existing generated-image safety model.
- The new regression tests explicitly reject `/backend-api/me`, conversation endpoints, unsafe sandbox paths, non-`file_` estuary IDs, no-candidate button polling, and global/stale button scans.
target.author: pdurlej

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
